Frame.purpose: SRL predicate frame anchored to a span in the token graph

property_notes.framework: Sense inventory: PROPBANK (verbal, transformer-srl port 8010) or NOMBANK (nominal, CogComp port 8011). Required when written by an SRL pass.
property_notes.sense: Roleset sense id e.g. 'attack.01'. Advisory-tier; omitted when upstream service does not provide it.
property_notes.sense_conf: Float confidence for sense. Advisory-tier; omitted when not provided.
property_notes.provisional: Boolean flag set true when frame_confidence < ingestion gating threshold.
property_notes.raw_role_count: Number of raw (pre-normalization) argument labels stored on PARTICIPANT edges. Advisory.

edges_out.note: Links verbal (PROPBANK) and nominal (NOMBANK) frames that describe the same situation. Confidence-weighted.

Signal.id_convention: signal sid from TTK, else sig_<doc>_<start>_<end>

TimexMention.purpose: Mention-level temporal span abstraction linked to canonical TIMEX

NUMERIC.purpose: Canonical numeric participant type, typically represented as a dynamic label on selected NamedEntity mentions

VALUE.purpose: Canonical value expression node/label used for quantified participants

EventMention.purpose: Mention-level event instantiation linked to canonical TEvent

relationships.PARTICIPANT: FrameArgument -> Frame (properties: type, prep?) [transitioning — see deprecated_relationships]
relationships.TRIGGERS: TagOccurrence -> TimexMention|TEvent|Signal (legacy direct TagOccurrence -> TIMEX tolerated during transition)
relationships.FRAME_DESCRIBES_EVENT: Frame -> TEvent (canonical replacement for DESCRIBES in this context)
relationships.ALIGNS_WITH: Frame -> Frame (optional; links PROPBANK and NOMBANK frames that describe the same situation; properties: confidence, alignment_key)
relationships.HAS_FRAME_ARGUMENT: FrameArgument -> Frame (canonical replacement for PARTICIPANT in this context)
relationships.EVENT_PARTICIPANT: Entity|NUMERIC|FrameArgument|VALUE -> TEvent|EventMention (canonical replacement for PARTICIPANT in participant context)
relationships.GLINK: TEvent -> TEvent (properties: id, relType, signalID, origin, rule) — grammatical link from TTK

source_hierarchy.notes: When sources disagree, treat runtime write paths plus applied migrations as authoritative. docs/schema.md is the maintained human contract and must be updated to match implemented writes.

identity_policy.notes: Temporal and event subgraphs should use the same doc_id type as AnnotatedText.id.

span_contract.note: legacy aliases are now disambiguated; do not reuse an alias across token and character spaces.

DESCRIBES.reason: Overloaded: used for both Frame->TEvent and Keyword->Document semantics. Split into semantically precise types.

PARTICIPANT.reason: Overloaded: used for FrameArgument->Frame and Entity|NUMERIC|FrameArgument->TEvent semantics. Split into precise types.

PARTICIPATES_IN.reason: Overloaded: used for both frame-attachment and mention-attachment token traversals. Split into explicit read/write contracts.

TRIGGERS.note: New writes must target TimexMention for temporal expressions; direct TagOccurrence -> TIMEX edges are legacy fallback only.

GLINK.note: Grammatical link extracted by TTK. Additive: only links pre-existing TEvent nodes.

argument_type_vocabulary.description: Controlled vocabulary for FrameArgument.argumentType. Maps PropBank ARGM modifier codes to stable semantic role category names. This vocabulary is the canonical reference; the CASE expression in EventEnrichmentPhase.add_non_core_participants_to_event must match exactly.

NUMERIC.description: NUMERIC is a deprecated dynamic label applied to selected NamedEntity mentions as a legacy fallback during canonical VALUE migration. Write paths are suppressed (fill_numeric_labels=False). Remaining instances are removed by migration 0019 (operator-triggered). Canonical queries must prefer :VALUE node targets and Entity-backed participants instead.
NUMERIC.query_guidance: Do not add new queries against :NUMERIC. For numeric or quantified arguments, query canonical :VALUE nodes or Entity-backed participants. Treat existing :NUMERIC reads as legacy fallback inventory targeted for removal.

VALUE.description: VALUE is a canonical node type for value expressions produced by the NER and enrichment pipeline (created by RefinementPhase.materialize_canonical_value_nodes()). It participates in EVENT_PARTICIPANT relationships to represent quantified arguments. Each VALUE node has a type classification (PERCENT, MONEY, QUANTITY, CARDINAL, ORDINAL, DATE, DURATION, NUMERIC, or OTHER). NOTE: Distinct from the deprecated VALUE dynamic label that was applied to selected NamedEntity nodes — that label is write-suppressed and targeted for removal by migration 0019.
VALUE.query_guidance: Query by label :VALUE for canonical value expression nodes; use v.type for filtering by value category. Check migration 0016 for TYPE governance constraints. Do not confuse this node type with the deprecated :VALUE dynamic label on NamedEntity nodes.

migration_manifest.description: Authoritative ordered list of migration files. CI validates that on-disk files match this manifest.

Evidence.notes: Evidence nodes are created with MERGE (evidence:Evidence {id: id(r), type: r.type}) — the id(r) value is non-deterministic across Neo4j restarts, making this node layer structurally unreliable. No maintained read-side consumers exist. The layer is retained in schema_tiers.legacy for backward-compatibility with old datasets; do not extend it.
Evidence.deprecation_path: retain_undisturbed — no migration required; layer rots passively. Remove in a future major-version alongside its paired Relationship nodes.

Relationship.notes: Relationship nodes are paired with Evidence nodes and share the same id(r) non-deterministic identity problem. Only writer is TextProcessor.build_relationships_inferred_graph. No maintained read-side consumers. Do not build new queries or features against this label.
Relationship.deprecation_path: retain_undisturbed — controlled by the same passive-retention policy as Evidence. Remove alongside Evidence in a future major-version.

diagnostics.name: TagOccurrence
diagnostics.query: MATCH (n:TagOccurrence) RETURN count(n) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: NamedEntity
diagnostics.query: MATCH (n:NamedEntity) RETURN count(n) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: FrameArgument
diagnostics.query: MATCH (n:FrameArgument) RETURN count(n) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: FrameArgument_with_headTokenIndex
diagnostics.query: MATCH (f:FrameArgument) WHERE exists(f.headTokenIndex) RETURN count(f) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: Frame
diagnostics.query: MATCH (n:Frame) RETURN count(n) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: Antecedent
diagnostics.query: MATCH (n:Antecedent) RETURN count(n) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: CorefMention
diagnostics.query: MATCH (n:CorefMention) RETURN count(n) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: IS_DEPENDENT_rels
diagnostics.query: MATCH ()-[r:IS_DEPENDENT]-() RETURN count(r) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: PARTICIPATES_IN_rels
diagnostics.query: MATCH ()-[r:PARTICIPATES_IN]-() RETURN count(r) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: IS_RELATED_TO_rels
diagnostics.query: MATCH ()-[r:IS_RELATED_TO]-() RETURN count(r) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: Evidence_nodes
diagnostics.query: MATCH (n:Evidence) RETURN count(n) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: Relationship_nodes
diagnostics.query: MATCH (n:Relationship) RETURN count(n) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: Frame_missing_start_tok
diagnostics.query: MATCH (n:Frame) WHERE n.start_tok IS NULL RETURN count(n) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: FrameArgument_missing_start_tok
diagnostics.query: MATCH (n:FrameArgument) WHERE n.start_tok IS NULL RETURN count(n) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: NamedEntity_missing_start_tok
diagnostics.query: MATCH (n:NamedEntity) WHERE n.start_tok IS NULL RETURN count(n) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: Antecedent_missing_start_tok
diagnostics.query: MATCH (n:Antecedent) WHERE n.start_tok IS NULL RETURN count(n) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: CorefMention_missing_start_tok
diagnostics.query: MATCH (n:CorefMention) WHERE n.start_tok IS NULL RETURN count(n) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: NounChunk_missing_start_tok
diagnostics.query: MATCH (n:NounChunk) WHERE n.start_tok IS NULL RETURN count(n) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: Signal_nodes
diagnostics.query: MATCH (n:Signal) RETURN count(n) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: CLINK_rels
diagnostics.query: MATCH ()-[r:CLINK]->() RETURN count(r) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: SLINK_rels
diagnostics.query: MATCH ()-[r:SLINK]->() RETURN count(r) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: Legacy_Tag_REFERS_TO_rels
diagnostics.query: MATCH (:TagOccurrence)-[r:REFERS_TO]->(:Tag) RETURN count(r) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: HAS_LEMMA_rels
diagnostics.query: MATCH (:TagOccurrence)-[r:HAS_LEMMA]->(:Tag) RETURN count(r) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: EventMention_missing_factuality
diagnostics.query: MATCH (em:EventMention) WHERE em.factuality IS NULL OR trim(toString(em.factuality)) = '' RETURN count(em) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: EventMention_factuality_missing_attribution
diagnostics.query: MATCH (em:EventMention) WHERE em.factuality IS NOT NULL AND trim(toString(em.factuality)) <> '' AND (em.factualitySource IS NULL OR trim(toString(em.factualitySource)) = '' OR em.factualityConfidence IS NULL) RETURN count(em) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: Factuality_alignment_violations
diagnostics.query: MATCH (em:EventMention)-[:REFERS_TO]->(te:TEvent) WHERE em.factuality IS NOT NULL AND trim(toString(em.factuality)) <> '' AND te.factuality IS NOT NULL AND trim(toString(te.factuality)) <> '' AND toUpper(toString(em.factuality)) <> toUpper(toString(te.factuality)) AND coalesce(te.factualityConflictFlag, false) = false RETURN count(*) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: TIMEX_missing_start_tok
diagnostics.query: MATCH (n:TIMEX) WHERE n.start_tok IS NULL RETURN count(n) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: TimexMention_missing_doc_id
diagnostics.query: MATCH (n:TimexMention) WHERE n.doc_id IS NULL OR trim(toString(n.doc_id)) = '' RETURN count(n) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: TimexMention_missing_span_coordinates
diagnostics.query: MATCH (n:TimexMention) WHERE n.start_tok IS NULL OR n.end_tok IS NULL RETURN count(n) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: TimexMention_missing_refers_to_timex
diagnostics.query: MATCH (n:TimexMention) WHERE NOT EXISTS { MATCH (n)-[:REFERS_TO]->(:TIMEX) } RETURN count(n) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: DCT_TimexMention_count
diagnostics.query: MATCH (n:TimexMention) WHERE toUpper(trim(coalesce(toString(n.tid), ''))) IN ['DCT', 'T0'] RETURN count(n) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: Signal_missing_start_tok
diagnostics.query: MATCH (n:Signal) WHERE n.start_tok IS NULL RETURN count(n) AS cnt

diagnostics.name: TEvent_missing_start_tok
diagnostics.query: MATCH (n:TEvent) WHERE n.start_tok IS NULL RETURN count(n) AS cnt
